purpose: PreToolUse hook on Task. Blocks worker dispatches until the user has explicitly approved the master's plan. Per .claude/skills/p-track/SKILL.md Step 11E: master writes a `permission`-level log entry 'Plan ready for review: ...', halts, then on user approval writes an `info`-level 'Approval granted ...'. This hook extracts the dashboard ID from the worker prompt's progress-file path (only acts when prompt contains TEMPLATE_VERSION:, i.e. is a real worker dispatch), reads logs.json, finds the latest 'Plan ready for review' permission entry, then verifies an 'Approval granted' info entry exists at a higher index. Repair/verification dispatches inherit the original approval — the gate is one-shot.
gotchas: Only gates prompts containing TEMPLATE_VERSION: (skips research/explore Task calls)
Once 'Approval granted' lands after the latest 'Plan ready for review', every subsequent dispatch passes — no per-task re-approval
Block message points the master at .claude/skills/p-track/SKILL.md Step 11E for the protocol
Uses jq's `to_entries[] | .key` indexing — depends on logs.json having an `entries[]` array shape
Block if logs.json missing — prevents dispatching before plan is even written
patterns: Index-aware log search: find latest permission entry, then check info entries at higher indices
Hook-enforced protocol gate referenced by skill prose ('NON-NEGOTIABLE')
conventions: Approval signal is the literal string 'Approval granted' (case-insensitive) at info level
